Title: Tropinone synthesis via an atypical polyketide synthase and P450-mediated cyclization

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase-like
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root preferentially expressed, especially lateral roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In A. belladonna roots/lateral roots; transgenically expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS in A. belladonna; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves; in vitro assays at pH 5.0-9.0 with optimum product selectivity near pH 8
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly uses the unconjugated N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium cation as a starter substrate and performs two rounds of malonyl-CoA-dependent decarboxylative condensation to form 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream; immediate precursor-forming step before tropinone ring closure
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically important specialized metabolites; medicinal alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots in A. belladonna biosynthesis context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key committed intermediate-forming step for tropinone biosynthesis; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid and thereby supports accumulation of tropinone and downstream tropane alkaloids; silencing greatly reduces tropinone, hyoscyamine, scopolamine, and calystegines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing in A. belladonna; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli-derived purified protein; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: A. belladonna transcriptome assembly and transcript abundance analysis from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ transient expression vector in N. benthamiana; promoter type not specified
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Its product is unstable and can spontaneously yield hygrine and cuscohygrine, which may divert metabolic flux away from tropinone
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering tropane alkaloid biosynthesis in non-tropane hosts, especially when stacked with AbPMT2, AbMPO2, and AbCYP82M3; flux loss to hygrine/cuscohygrine must be managed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Atypical/noncanonical type III PKS because it directly accepts a non-CoA-linked N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium starter substrate; does not itself make tropinone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPYKS was identified as a root-preferential type III PKS required for tropane alkaloid biosynthesis in A. belladonna. 2. Silencing AbPYKS reduced 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid by 92% and tropinone by 86%, along with downstream hyoscyamine, scopolamine, and calystegines. 3. Recombinant AbPYKS directly converts N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium cation plus malonyl-CoA into 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid through a noncanonical PKS mechanism. 4. AbPYKS alone does not produce tropinone, indicating a second enzyme is required.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tropinone synthase / bicyclic ring closure step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-preferential, significantly elevated in roots/lateral roots versus non-root tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In A. belladonna roots; transgenically expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS in A. belladonna; transient expression in N. benthamiana; microsomal assays in yeast require NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid to tropinone through P450-mediated cyclization/ring closure; additional dehydration, intramolecular condensation, and decarboxylation occur without extra enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream; terminal bicyclic ring-forming step to tropinone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically important specialized metabolites; medicinal alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots in A. belladonna biosynthesis context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for tropinone formation; likely bottleneck in engineered N. benthamiana system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tropinone formation from 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid; silencing lowers tropinone and downstream tropanes while increasing substrate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression/gene stacking in N. benthamiana; recombinant expression in yeast microsomes with enzyme assay; VIGS in A. belladonna; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: A. belladonna transcriptome assembly and transcript abundance analysis from Medicinal Plant Genomics Resource
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ transient expression vector in N. benthamiana; promoter type not specified
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In engineering, activity appears limiting relative to AbPYKS, potentially constraining tropinone yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical gene for reconstructing tropinone biosynthesis in heterologous hosts; may require optimization with suitable P450 reductase pairing and co-expression with upstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only candidate P450 among four root-preferential P450s that produced tropinone in N. benthamiana; proposed mechanism may involve hydroxylation at C-5 followed by dehydration and cyclization, but intermediates were not directly detected
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbCYP82M3 (formerly AbP450-3127) was identified as the cytochrome P450 responsible for tropinone synthesis. 2. Coexpression of AbCYP82M3 with AbPMT2, AbMPO2, and AbPYKS in N. benthamiana produced tropinone, whereas other candidate P450s did not. 3. Yeast microsomes expressing AbCYP82M3 converted 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid to tropinone in an NADPH-dependent manner. 4. Silencing AbCYP82M3 in A. belladonna reduced tropinone and downstream tropanes and increased the immediate substrate, confirming its in planta role.
